result:
**Goal: Add a Cortical Neural Dynamics & Seizure Propagation simulation mode.**

This is the brain equivalent of the existing Cardiac Electrophysiology mode — a natural scientific parallel that fills a clear gap. The project has heart electrophysiology but no detailed brain electrophysiology despite having a basic spiking neural network mode.

The mode would model a 2D cortical sheet with excitatory/inhibitory (E/I) neuron populations using Wilson-Cowan dynamics, STDP synaptic plasticity, emergent neural oscillations (theta 4-8Hz, alpha 8-13Hz, beta 13-30Hz, gamma 30-80Hz), GABAergic inhibition balance, focal seizure initiation from E/I imbalance, seizure wavefront propagation across cortex, spreading cortical depression, and anticonvulsant drug mechanics. Three views: spatial cortical activation map with E/I cell coloring and wavefront glyphs, real-time EEG trace (multi-channel scrolling with frequency band decomposition), and sparkline time-series for 10 metrics. Six presets: Normal Resting State (alpha-dominant idle), Gamma Burst Working Memory (task-evoked high-frequency), Focal Seizure Onset (local E/I collapse spreading outward), Generalized Tonic-Clonic (whole-cortex hypersynchrony), Spreading Depression (slow depolarization wave with silenced wake), and Drug Intervention (apply GABAergic anticonvulsant mid-seizure).
